title: GROUP BY ALL started consuming much more RAM
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
Queries with aggregation started reporting 100x more RAM usage than earlier versions:
v25.6.7.23
```
SELECT c,b FROM test2e8 GROUP BY ALL
# First query
100 rows in set. Elapsed: 0.210 sec. Processed 200.00 million rows, 4.18 GB (952.96 million rows/s., 19.92 GB/s.)
Peak memory usage: 4.32 MiB.
# Later queries
100 rows in set. Elapsed: 0.191 sec. Processed 200.00 million rows, 4.18 GB (1.05 billion rows/s., 21.85 GB/s.)
Peak memory usage: 380.96 KiB.
```
v25.7.1.3997
```
SELECT c,b FROM test2e8 GROUP BY ALL
# First query
100 rows in set. Elapsed: 0.212 sec. Processed 200.00 million rows, 4.18 GB (941.38 million rows/s., 19.67 GB/s.)
Peak memory usage: 4.35 MiB.
# Later queries
100 rows in set. Elapsed: 0.191 sec. Processed 200.00 million rows, 4.18 GB (1.05 billion rows/s., 21.86 GB/s.)
Peak memory usage: 417.00 KiB.
```
v25.8.1.2656
```
SELECT c,b FROM test2e8 GROUP BY ALL
# First query
100 rows in set. Elapsed: 0.290 sec. Processed 200.00 million rows, 4.18 GB (688.72 million rows/s., 14.39 GB/s.)
Peak memory usage: 149.10 MiB.
# Later queries
100 rows in set. Elapsed: 0.256 sec. Processed 200.00 million rows, 4.18 GB (780.41 million rows/s., 16.31 GB/s.)
Peak memory usage: 142.90 MiB.
```
The same can be observed in current `master` branch.
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
25.8
### How to reproduce
```sql
CREATE TABLE test2e8
ENGINE = MergeTree
ORDER BY a
AS SELECT
toString(number % 1000) AS a,
toString(number % 100) AS b,
toString(number % 10) AS c,
toString(number) AS d
FROM numbers_mt(2E8);
SELECT c,b FROM test2e8 GROUP BY ALL;
```
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test2e9 (id INT, value DOUBLE PRECISION, name VARCHAR(255));
CREATE TABLE test2e10 AS SELECT id, value, name FROM test2e9 WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT name, COUNT(*) FROM test2e10 GROUP BY name;
SELECT id, AVG(value) FROM test2e9 GROUP BY id;
key_bug_features: GROUP BY ALL causing excessive RAM usage
